title: Measles Outbreak Kills 70, Infects 1,000 in Labado Displacement Area, East Darfur Since March 2026
detail: A measles outbreak in the Labado displacement area of East Darfur has killed approximately 70 people and infected around 1,000 others since March 2026, human rights groups reported on April 28. Labado hosts large numbers of internally displaced persons from across Darfur who fled the RSF's ethnic violence campaign since 2023. The outbreak is consistent with collapsed healthcare infrastructure across Darfur — RSF occupation has destroyed or rendered non-functional over 40% of Sudan's health facilities, and the WHO documented that 65% of Darfur's population has been deprived of basic medical services. Vaccination campaigns have been impossible in most RSF-controlled Darfur areas due to active conflict, access denial, and targeted attacks on healthcare workers. The Labado outbreak adds to a constellation of disease crises sweeping Sudan's displacement camps: cholera, malaria, and acute watery diarrhea outbreaks have been documented at multiple IDP sites since 2023. UNICEF has warned that Sudan's children face overlapping threats of starvation, disease, and violence — 6 million children are displaced and 4.9 million children under 5 are acutely malnourished. WHO's Sudan Country Office has limited access to RSF-controlled Darfur, making verified outbreak figures extremely difficult to obtain.

media.caption: Human rights groups warn of dire conditions in Sudan's Darfur including measles outbreak killing 70 in Labado displacement area

title: Fire at Central Darfur Displacement Camp Kills 4 Including Children, Injures 12
detail: A fire swept through a displacement camp in Central Darfur on April 28, 2026, killing at least 4 people including children and injuring 12 others. Exact details of the fire's cause and precise camp location are unconfirmed; the camp is believed to be in the Zalingei area of Central Darfur, which hosts large IDP populations including those displaced by the SAF drone strike on Al-Hamidiyah camp the previous day (April 27, 6 killed). Fires at IDP camps have been a recurring tragedy in Sudan's displacement crisis: camps are densely packed with shelters made from tarps, cardboard, and lightweight materials that are highly flammable, while fire-fighting infrastructure and emergency services are essentially non-existent. Access for humanitarian organizations to document and respond to such incidents in RSF-controlled Central Darfur is severely limited. Central Darfur has been under RSF control since mid-2023, with Zalingei serving as the state capital.

media.caption: Fire at Central Darfur displacement camp kills 4 including children and injures 12 on April 28, 2026
